claim: Claude was trained specifically to practice being an agent through reinforcement learning on long-running tasks where it takes many steps and uses tools before giving final answers
actionable: Understand that Claude's agentic capabilities come from specialized training, making it naturally suited for multi-step reasoning and tool use compared to models without this training

claim: Coding is a fundamental skill that enables agents to accomplish other tasks; strong coding agents can do web search, planning, and other non-coding work effectively
actionable: If building a multi-capability agent, prioritize strong coding abilities first; this will unlock effectiveness in other domains

claim: Use single-shot prompting with Claude Code SDK for simplicity, then only add complexity (workflows, multi-agent systems) as needed
actionable: Start with single-shot Claude Code SDK prompts before layering in multi-agent orchestration, subagent delegation, or parallel processing

claim: Tools and MCPs should be designed from the model's perspective (like a UI for the model) rather than mirroring API endpoints one-to-one
actionable: When creating MCP tools or APIs for agents, design them as the agent would experience them (showing all related info at once) not as your backend API is structured

claim: Multi-agent systems work best with simple orchestration; overbuilt systems with too much agent-to-agent communication waste context and time without making progress
actionable: Keep multi-agent communication minimal; design systems to parallelize work rather than have agents constantly reporting back to each other

claim: Subagents can be used to offload context-heavy work (like searching for a specific code implementation) while protecting the main agent's context window
actionable: When a subtask will consume many tokens but the result is small, delegate to a subagent rather than having the main agent process all tokens

claim: Computer use combined with agent code-writing abilities enables agents to verify their own work by opening and testing what they built
actionable: Combine computer use capabilities with coding agents so they can test their own web apps, run code, and catch bugs without human QA
